# frozen_string_literal: true

class StatusFilter
  attr_reader :status, :account

  def initialize(status, account)
    @status              = status
    @account             = account
  end

  def filtered?
    return false if !account.nil? && account.id == status.account_id

    blocked_by_policy? || (account_present? && filtered_status?) || silenced_account?
  end

  def filter_state_for_quote
    if !account.nil? && account.id == status.account_id
      nil
    elsif blocked_by_policy?
      'unauthorized'
    elsif account_present? && blocking_domain?
      'blocked_domain'
    elsif account_present? && blocking_account?
      'blocked_account'
    elsif account_present? && muting_account?
      'muted_account'
    end
  end

  private

  def account_present?
    !account.nil?
  end

  def filtered_status?
    blocking_account? || blocking_domain? || muting_account?
  end

  def blocking_account?
    account.blocking?(status.account_id)
  end

  def blocking_domain?
    account.domain_blocking?(status.account_domain)
  end

  def muting_account?
    account.muting?(status.account_id)
  end

  def silenced_account?
    status_account_silenced? && !account_following_status_account?
  end

  def status_account_silenced?
    status.account.silenced?
  end

  def account_following_status_account?
    account&.following?(status.account_id)
  end

  def blocked_by_policy?
    !policy_allows_show?
  end

  def policy_allows_show?
    StatusPolicy.new(account, status).show?
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

class StatusFinder
  attr_reader :url

  def initialize(url)
    @url = url
  end

  def status
    verify_action!

    raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound unless TagManager.instance.local_url?(url)

    case recognized_params[:controller]
    when 'statuses'
      Status.find(recognized_params[:id])
    else
      raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    end
  end

  private

  def recognized_params
    Rails.application.routes.recognize_path(url)
  end

  def verify_action!
    raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound unless recognized_params[:action] == 'show'
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

class StatusPolicy < ApplicationPolicy
  def show?
    return false if author.unavailable?

    if requires_mention?
      owned? || mention_exists?
    elsif private?
      owned? || following_author? || mention_exists?
    else
      current_account.nil? || (!author_blocking? && !author_blocking_domain?)
    end
  end

  def quote?
    show? && !blocking_author? && record.quote_policy_for_account(current_account) != :denied
  end

  def reblog?
    !requires_mention? && (!private? || owned?) && show? && !blocking_author?
  end

  def favourite?
    show? && !blocking_author?
  end

  def destroy?
    owned?
  end

  alias unreblog? destroy?

  def update?
    owned?
  end

  private

  def requires_mention?
    record.direct_visibility? || record.limited_visibility?
  end

  def owned?
    author.id == current_account&.id
  end

  def private?
    record.private_visibility?
  end

  def mention_exists?
    return false if current_account.nil?

    if record.mentions.loaded?
      record.mentions.any? { |mention| mention.account_id == current_account.id }
    else
      record.mentions.exists?(account: current_account)
    end
  end

  def author_blocking_domain?
    return false if current_account.nil? || current_account.domain.nil?

    author.domain_blocking?(current_account.domain)
  end

  def blocking_author?
    return false if current_account.nil?

    current_account.blocking?(author)
  end

  def author_blocking?
    return false if current_account.nil?

    current_account.blocked_by?(author)
  end

  def following_author?
    return false if current_account.nil?

    current_account.following?(author)
  end

  def author
    record.account
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

class BatchedRemoveStatusService < BaseService
  include Redisable

  # Delete multiple statuses and reblogs of them as efficiently as possible
  # @param [Enumerable<Status>] statuses An array of statuses
  # @param [Hash] options
  # @option [Boolean] :skip_side_effects Do not modify feeds and send updates to streaming API
  def call(statuses, **options)
    ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(
      records: statuses,
      associations: options[:skip_side_effects] ? :reblogs : [:account, :tags, reblogs: :account]
    ).call

    statuses_and_reblogs = statuses.flat_map { |status| [status] + status.reblogs }

    # The conversations for direct visibility statuses also need
    # to be manually updated. This part is not efficient but we
    # rely on direct visibility statuses being relatively rare.
    statuses_with_account_conversations = statuses.select(&:direct_visibility?)

    ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(
      records: statuses_with_account_conversations,
      associations: [mentions: :account]
    ).call

    statuses_with_account_conversations.each(&:unlink_from_conversations!)

    # We do not batch all deletes into one to avoid having a long-running
    # transaction lock the database, but we use the delete method instead
    # of destroy to avoid all callbacks. We rely on foreign keys to
    # cascade the delete faster without loading the associations.
    statuses_and_reblogs.each_slice(50) { |slice| Status.unscoped.where(id: slice.pluck(:id)).delete_all }

    # Since we skipped all callbacks, we also need to manually
    # deindex the statuses
    if Chewy.enabled?
      Chewy.strategy.current.update(StatusesIndex, statuses_and_reblogs)
      Chewy.strategy.current.update(PublicStatusesIndex, statuses_and_reblogs)
    end

    return if options[:skip_side_effects]

    # Batch by source account
    statuses_and_reblogs.group_by(&:account_id).each_value do |account_statuses|
      account = account_statuses.first.account

      next unless account

      unpush_from_home_timelines(account, account_statuses)
      unpush_from_list_timelines(account, account_statuses)
    end

    # Cannot be batched
    @status_id_cutoff = Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(2.weeks.ago)
    redis.pipelined do |pipeline|
      statuses.each do |status|
        unpush_from_public_timelines(status, pipeline)
      end
    end
  end

  private

  def unpush_from_home_timelines(account, statuses)
    account.followers_for_local_distribution.includes(:user).reorder(nil).find_each do |follower|
      statuses.each do |status|
        FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_home(follower, status)
      end
    end
  end

  def unpush_from_list_timelines(account, statuses)
    account.lists_for_local_distribution.select(:id, :account_id).includes(account: :user).reorder(nil).find_each do |list|
      statuses.each do |status|
        FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_list(list, status)
      end
    end
  end

  def unpush_from_public_timelines(status, pipeline)
    return unless status.public_visibility? && status.id > @status_id_cutoff

    payload = { event: :delete, payload: status.id.to_s }.to_json

    pipeline.publish('timeline:public', payload)
    pipeline.publish(status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local' : 'timeline:public:remote', payload)

    if status.media_attachments.any?
      pipeline.publish('timeline:public:media', payload)
      pipeline.publish(status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local:media' : 'timeline:public:remote:media', payload)
    end

    status.tags.map { |tag| tag.name.downcase }.each do |hashtag|
      pipeline.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag}", payload)
      pipeline.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag}:local", payload) if status.local?
    end
  end
end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	class StatusReachFinder
4	  # @param [Status] status
5	  # @param [Hash] options
6	  # @option options [Boolean] :unsafe
7	  def initialize(status, options = {})
8	    @status  = status
9	    @options = options
10	  end
11	
12	  def inboxes
13	    (reached_account_inboxes + followers_inboxes + relay_inboxes).uniq
14	  end
15	
16	  private
17	
18	  def reached_account_inboxes
19	    scope = Account.where(id: reached_account_ids)
20	    inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
21	  end
22	
23	  def reached_account_ids
24	    # When the status is a reblog, there are no interactions with it
25	    # directly, we assume all interactions are with the original one
26	
27	    if @status.reblog?
28	      [reblog_of_account_id]
29	    else
30	      [
31	        replied_to_account_id,
32	        reblog_of_account_id,

# frozen_string_literal: true

class StatusReachFinder
  # @param [Status] status
  # @param [Hash] options
  # @option options [Boolean] :unsafe
  def initialize(status, options = {})
    @status  = status
    @options = options
  end

  def inboxes
    (reached_account_inboxes + followers_inboxes + relay_inboxes).uniq
  end

  private

  def reached_account_inboxes
    scope = Account.where(id: reached_account_ids)
    inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
  end

  def reached_account_ids
    # When the status is a reblog, there are no interactions with it
    # directly, we assume all interactions are with the original one

    if @status.reblog?
      [reblog_of_account_id]
    else
      [
        replied_to_account_id,
        reblog_of_account_id,
        quote_of_account_id,
        mentioned_account_ids,
        reblogs_account_ids,
        quotes_account_ids,
        favourites_account_ids,
        replies_account_ids,
      ].tap do |arr|
        arr.flatten!
        arr.compact!
        arr.uniq!
      end
    end
  end

  def replied_to_account_id
    @status.in_reply_to_account_id if distributable?
  end

  def quote_of_account_id
    @status.quote&.quoted_account_id
  end

  def reblog_of_account_id
    @status.reblog.account_id if @status.reblog?
  end

  def mentioned_account_ids
    @status.mentions.pluck(:account_id)
  end

  # Beware: Quotes can be created without the author having had access to the status
  def quotes_account_ids
    @status.quotes.pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
  end

  # Beware: Reblogs can be created without the author having had access to the status
  def reblogs_account_ids
    @status.reblogs.rewhere(deleted_at: [nil, @status.deleted_at]).pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
  end

  # Beware: Favourites can be created without the author having had access to the status
  def favourites_account_ids
    @status.favourites.pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
  end

  # Beware: Replies can be created without the author having had access to the status
  def replies_account_ids
    @status.replies.pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
  end

  def followers_inboxes
    scope = followers_scope
    inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
  end

  def relay_inboxes
    if @status.public_visibility?
      Relay.enabled.pluck(:inbox_url)
    else
      []
    end
  end

  def distributable?
    @status.public_visibility? || @status.unlisted_visibility?
  end

  def unsafe?
    @options[:unsafe]
  end

  def followers_scope
    if @status.in_reply_to_local_account? && distributable?
      @status.account.followers.or(@status.thread.account.followers.not_domain_blocked_by_account(@status.account))
    elsif @status.direct_visibility? || @status.limited_visibility?
      Account.none
    else
      @status.account.followers
    end
  end

  def inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
    scope.merge!(Account.without_suspended) unless unsafe?
    scope.inboxes
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

class ActivityPub::DistributionWorker < ActivityPub::RawDistributionWorker
  # Skip followers synchronization for accounts with a large number of followers,
  # as this is expensive and people with very large amounts of followers
  # necessarily have less control over them to begin with
  MAX_FOLLOWERS_FOR_SYNCHRONIZATION = 25_000

  # Distribute a new status or an edit of a status to all the places
  # where the status is supposed to go or where it was interacted with
  def perform(status_id)
    @status  = Status.find(status_id)
    @account = @status.account

    distribute!
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    true
  end

  protected

  def inboxes
    @inboxes ||= StatusReachFinder.new(@status).inboxes
  end

  def payload
    @payload ||= serialize_payload(@status, activity_serializer, serializer_options.merge(signer: @account)).to_json
  end

  def activity_serializer
    @status.reblog? ? ActivityPub::AnnounceNoteSerializer : ActivityPub::CreateNoteSerializer
  end

  def serializer_options
    {}
  end

  def options
    { 'synchronize_followers' => @status.private_visibility? && @account.followers_count < MAX_FOLLOWERS_FOR_SYNCHRONIZATION }
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

class Vacuum::StatusesVacuum
  include Redisable

  def initialize(retention_period)
    @retention_period = retention_period
  end

  def perform
    vacuum_statuses! if @retention_period.present?
  end

  private

  def vacuum_statuses!
    statuses_scope.in_batches do |statuses|
      # Side-effects not covered by foreign keys, such
      # as the search index, must be handled first.
      statuses.direct_visibility
        .includes(mentions: :account)
        .find_each(&:unlink_from_conversations!)
      if Chewy.enabled?
        remove_from_index(statuses.ids, 'chewy:queue:StatusesIndex')
        remove_from_index(statuses.ids, 'chewy:queue:PublicStatusesIndex')
      end

      # Foreign keys take care of most associated records for us.
      # Media attachments will be orphaned.
      statuses.delete_all
    end
  end

  def statuses_scope
    Status.unscoped.kept
      .joins(:account).merge(Account.remote)
      .where(statuses: { id: ...retention_period_as_id })
  end

  def retention_period_as_id
    Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(@retention_period.ago, with_random: false)
  end

  def remove_from_index(status_ids, index)
    with_redis { |redis| redis.sadd(index, status_ids) }
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

# == Schema Information
#
# Table name: account_statuses_cleanup_policies
#
#  id                 :bigint(8)        not null, primary key
#  enabled            :boolean          default(TRUE), not null
#  keep_direct        :boolean          default(TRUE), not null
#  keep_media         :boolean          default(FALSE), not null
#  keep_pinned        :boolean          default(TRUE), not null
#  keep_polls         :boolean          default(FALSE), not null
#  keep_self_bookmark :boolean          default(TRUE), not null
#  keep_self_fav      :boolean          default(TRUE), not null
#  min_favs           :integer
#  min_reblogs        :integer
#  min_status_age     :integer          default(1209600), not null
#  created_at         :datetime         not null
#  updated_at         :datetime         not null
#  account_id         :bigint(8)        not null
#
class AccountStatusesCleanupPolicy < ApplicationRecord
  include Redisable

  ALLOWED_MIN_STATUS_AGE = [
    1.week.seconds,
    2.weeks.seconds,
    1.month.seconds,
    2.months.seconds,
    3.months.seconds,
    6.months.seconds,
    1.year.seconds,
    2.years.seconds,
  ].freeze

  EXCEPTION_BOOLS      = %w(keep_direct keep_pinned keep_polls keep_media keep_self_fav keep_self_bookmark).freeze
  EXCEPTION_THRESHOLDS = %w(min_favs min_reblogs).freeze

  # Depending on the cleanup policy, the query to discover the next
  # statuses to delete my get expensive if the account has a lot of old
  # statuses otherwise excluded from deletion by the other exceptions.
  #
  # Therefore, `EARLY_SEARCH_CUTOFF` is meant to be the maximum number of
  # old statuses to be considered for deletion prior to checking exceptions.
  #
  # This is used in `compute_cutoff_id` to provide a `max_id` to
  # `statuses_to_delete`.
  EARLY_SEARCH_CUTOFF = 5_000

  belongs_to :account

  validates :min_status_age, inclusion: { in: ALLOWED_MIN_STATUS_AGE }
  validates :min_favs, numericality: { greater_than_or_equal_to: 1, allow_nil: true }
  validates :min_reblogs, numericality: { greater_than_or_equal_to: 1, allow_nil: true }
  validate :validate_local_account

  before_save :update_last_inspected

  def statuses_to_delete(limit = 50, max_id = nil, min_id = nil)
    scope = account_statuses
    scope.merge!(old_enough_scope(max_id))
    scope = scope.where(id: min_id..) if min_id.present?
    scope.merge!(without_popular_scope) unless min_favs.nil? && min_reblogs.nil?
    scope.merge!(without_direct_scope) if keep_direct?
    scope.merge!(without_pinned_scope) if keep_pinned?
    scope.merge!(without_poll_scope) if keep_polls?
    scope.merge!(without_media_scope) if keep_media?
    scope.merge!(without_self_fav_scope) if keep_self_fav?
    scope.merge!(without_self_bookmark_scope) if keep_self_bookmark?

    scope.reorder(id: :asc).limit(limit)
  end

  # This computes a toot id such that:
  # - the toot would be old enough to be candidate for deletion
  # - there are at most EARLY_SEARCH_CUTOFF toots between the last inspected toot and this one
  #
  # The idea is to limit expensive SQL queries when an account has lots of toots excluded from
  # deletion, while not starting anew on each run.
  def compute_cutoff_id

content.text:
I now have a complete, cross-checked picture from the Sense index plus direct reads of the model, concerns, distribution, teardown, and guard code. Here is the audit.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Mastodon — a Rails 8 + React federated social server (ActivityPub). The Sense index shows 4,102 files / 17,571 symbols; Ruby is the backend (`app/`), React/TS is the web client (`app/javascript/`).

**Layout (backend, where the rework lives):**
- `app/models/` — ActiveRecord models + `app/models/concerns/` for shared mixins
- `app/services/` — business operations (post, remove, update, fan-out)
- `app/workers/` — Sidekiq async jobs (distribution, feeds, federation)
- `app/lib/` — domain logic (`activitypub/`, `feed_manager.rb`, finders, vacuum)
- `app/policies/` — Pundit authorization; `app/serializers/` — REST + ActivityPub output
- `app/controllers/` — REST API (`api/v1`, `api/v2`), ActivityPub, admin
- `lib/mastodon/cli/` — maintenance CLI; `spec/` — RSpec + Fabrication fixtures

**The central content model:** `Status` at `app/models/status.rb:34`. It is a top hub (Sense: 124–207 callers, "hub" role). Everything users post is a Status; feeds, notifications, federation, and moderation revolve around it. It is built on a stack of `Status::*` concerns (`app/models/concerns/status/`) and the cross-cutting concerns `Cacheable`, `Discard::Model`, `Paginable`, `RateLimitable`. Its closest collaborators: `Account` (`app/models/account.rb`), `StatusStat`, `StatusEdit`, `Conversation`, `Poll`, `MediaAttachment`, `Mention`, `Favourite`, `Bookmark`, `Quote`, `Tag`.

---

## Step 2 — The Status contract and what it is built on

**Model:** `app/models/status.rb:34`

**Concerns it includes (`app/models/status.rb:35-46`):**
| Concern | File | What it contributes |
|---|---|---|
| `Cacheable` | `status.rb:35` + `cache_associated` `status.rb:180` | fragment/association caching; `CACHEABLE_ASSOCIATIONS` `status.rb:48` |
| `Discard::Model` | `status.rb:36`, `discard_column = :deleted_at` `:66` | **soft-delete** — this is the teardown core |
| `Paginable` | `status.rb:37` | id-based pagination scopes |
| `RateLimitable` | `status.rb:38`, `rate_limit :64` | per-account post rate limiting |
| `Status::FaspConcern` | `concerns/status/fasp_concern.rb:3` | `after_commit` create/update/destroy → FASP federation announce hooks |
| `Status::FetchRepliesConcern` | `fetch_replies_concern.rb:3` | remote reply backfill scopes + `should_fetch_replies?` |
| `Status::SafeReblogInsert` | `safe_reblog_insert.rb:3` | overrides `_insert_record` to block reblogs of discarded statuses at SQL level |
| `Status::SearchConcern` | `search_concern.rb:3` | `indexable` scope, `searchable_by/_text/_properties` (Chewy/ES) |
| `Status::SnapshotConcern` | `snapshot_concern.rb:3` | `has_many :edits`; `build_snapshot`/`snapshot!` for edit history |
| `Status::ThreadingConcern` | `threading_concern.rb:3` | `ancestors`/`descendants`/`self_replies` + `permitted_statuses_from_ids` |
| `Status::Visibility` | `visibility.rb:3` | `enum :visibility` `:7`, `distributable?`/`hidden?`, `set_visibility` |
| `Status::InteractionPolicyConcern` | `interaction_policy_concern.rb:3` | `composed_of :quote_interaction_policy`, quote-permission logic |

**Associations other records hang off (`status.rb:75-118`):** `account`, `application`, `conversation`/`owned_conversation`, `preloadable_poll`/`poll`, `thread`+`replies` (self-join via `in_reply_to_id`), `reblog`+`reblogs` (self-join via `reblog_of_id`), `favourites`, `bookmarks`, `mentions`/`active_mentions`/`mentioned_accounts`, `media_attachments` (`dependent: :nullify`), `tags` (HABTM), `tagged_objects`, `quotes`/`quote`, `preview_cards_status`, `notification` (polymorphic `as: :activity`), `status_stat`, `trend` (`StatusTrend`), `edits` (from SnapshotConcern). Note the **mixed teardown semantics**: `:destroy` (favourites, bookmarks, reblogs, mentions, poll, quote, notification, edits), `:nullify` (media_attachments), `:delete` (preview_cards_status), `nil` (replies, status_stat, owned_conversation, trend).

**Lifecycle the contract guarantees (`status.rb:157-184`):** create-commit hooks (`store_uri`, `update_statistics`, `increment_counter_caches`, webhooks), `before_validation` chain (`set_reblog`/`set_conversation`/`set_local`/`set_visibility`), Snowflake ID assignment `around_create :171`, and **`before_destroy :unlink_from_conversations!, prepend: true` `:178`** (must run before `dependent: :destroy`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:** (1) `default_scope { recent.kept }` `:128` silently hides discarded rows everywhere — changing discard/visibility semantics changes every query; (2) teardown is **two-phase**: soft-discard (`discard_with_reblogs` `:400`) then optional hard `destroy!`, and some children rely on DB foreign-key cascade rather than Rails callbacks (see `BatchedRemoveStatusService`, `Vacuum`); (3) visibility is an enum with `limited`/`direct` special-cased throughout; (4) `proper`/`reblog` indirection (`:234`) — a reblog delegates content to its target.

## Step 3 — How a status is created and distributed

**Entry → persistence (inline, synchronous):**
1. `PostStatusService#call` `app/services/post_status_service.rb:42` (from `Api::V1::StatusesController#create`). Idempotency lock `:236`, media validation `:187`.
2. `#process_status!` `:84`: builds `account.statuses.new` `:85` → `ProcessMentionsService` `:86` → tag objects/quote attach `:89-90` → `Antispam` preflight `:92` → `@status.save!` inside a transaction `:97`. Model callbacks fire here (Snowflake id, conversation/visibility setup, counter caches, `store_uri`, FASP create-announce, webhooks).
3. `#postprocess_status!` `:162` enqueues the async fan-out.

**Distribution layers (all async via Sidekiq, enqueued at `post_status_service.rb:163-170`):**
| Layer | Worker → Service | Runs |
|---|---|---|
| Link preview | `LinkCrawlWorker` `:165` | async |
| **Local fan-out** | `DistributionWorker` `:166` → `FanOutOnWriteService#call` `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12` | async |
| Email digests | `EmailDistributionWorker` `:184` | async (delayed) |
| **Federation** | `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker` `:168` → `StatusReachFinder` inboxes → `ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker` | async |
| Poll expiry | `PollExpirationNotifyWorker` `:169` | async (scheduled) |
| Quote request | `ActivityPub::QuoteRequestWorker` `:170` | async |
| Trends | `Trends.tags.register` `:164` | inline |

**Inside `FanOutOnWriteService` (`fan_out_on_write_service.rb`):** race-condition guard `:38`, warms render cache `:170`, then by visibility `:50`: `deliver_to_self!`/`deliver_to_all_followers!`/`deliver_to_lists!` (push `FeedInsertWorker`), notifications (`notify_mentioned_accounts!` `:81` → `LocalNotificationWorker`; `notify_quoted_account!` `:75`), and public streaming via `redis.publish` `:146-163`. Feed writes go through `FeedManager` (`app/lib/feed_manager.rb`). Notifications materialize via `NotifyService` and `PushUpdateWorker`.

## Step 5 — How status identity & visibility are guarded

When code operates on a status it confirms **(a) it exists/current, (b) it is visible/distributable, (c) the actor is authorized**:

**Existence / currentness:**
- `default_scope { recent.kept }` `app/models/status.rb:128` — `kept` (Discard) auto-excludes `deleted_at IS NOT NULL`; `.unscoped` is the explicit opt-out used by teardown (`status.rb:402`, `batched_remove_status_service.rb:34`, `vacuum/statuses_vacuum.rb:35`).
- Workers re-fetch by id and rescue `RecordNotFound`: `DistributionWorker#perform` `app/workers/distribution_worker.rb:12`, `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker#perform` `app/workers/activitypub/distribution_worker.rb:16`.
- `FanOutOnWriteService#check_race_condition!` `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:38` — re-queues if `visibility` is nil (partial object).
- `Status::SafeReblogInsert._insert_record` `app/models/concerns/status/safe_reblog_insert.rb:20` — DB-level `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL` so no reblog of a discarded status can be inserted.
- Distributed lock `with_redis_lock("distribute:#{id}")` in both `DistributionWorker:9` and `RemoveStatusService:22` serializes create vs. delete.

**Visibility:**
- `Status::Visibility#distributable?` `app/models/concerns/status/visibility.rb:31` (public||unlisted), `#hidden?` `:27`, scopes `distributable_visibility`/`list_eligible_visibility`/`not_direct_visibility` `:12-14`. Used as gates in `FanOutOnWriteService#broadcastable?` `:188`, `RemoveStatusService#remove_from_public/#remove_from_media/#remove_from_hashtags` (`public_visibility?` checks `:124,135,144`), `StatusReachFinder#distributable?` `app/lib/status_reach_finder.rb:96`, `Status::FaspConcern` (`public_visibility?` `fasp_concern.rb:17,29,36`), `BatchedRemoveStatusService#unpush_from_public_timelines` `:83`.

**Authorization / per-viewer filtering:**
- `StatusPolicy#show?` `app/policies/status_policy.rb:4` — checks `author.unavailable?`, mention requirement, private/following, blocks/domain-blocks. Plus `reblog?`/`favourite?`/`quote?`/`destroy?`/`update?`.
- `StatusFilter#filtered?` `app/lib/status_filter.rb:11` — delegates to `StatusPolicy#show?` `:70` and layers block/domain-block/mute/silence. Drives thread visibility in `Status::ThreadingConcern#permitted_statuses_from_ids` `app/models/concerns/status/threading_concern.rb:14`.
- `StatusReachFinder#followers_scope` `:104` + `inboxes_without_suspended_for` `:114` — strips suspended accounts unless `unsafe:` (delete reach `remove_status_service.rb:98` deliberately passes `unsafe: true`).
- Quote permission: `Status::InteractionPolicyConcern#quote_policy_for_account` `interaction_policy_concern.rb:20`.

**Identity resolution (only "real, local, current" objects act):** `StatusFinder#status` `app/lib/status_finder.rb:10` (verifies route + local URL), `ActivityPub::Activity#status_from_uri` `app/lib/activitypub/activity.rb:70`, `ResolveURLService#process_local_url` `app/services/resolve_url_service.rb:93`.

## Step 6 — Blast radius of the contract change

Sense `sense_blast` rates `Status` **high risk** — 153 direct callers, **317 affected symbols across 106 files**, 1,442 edges. Ranked by likelihood the rework (storage / visibility / teardown) breaks them:

**🔴 Critical — most likely to break, hardest to detect:**
1. **`BatchedRemoveStatusService#call`** `batched_remove_status_service.rb:34` & **`Vacuum::StatusesVacuum`** `vacuum/statuses_vacuum.rb:30` — **bypass Rails callbacks and `delete_all`, relying on DB foreign-key cascade + manual `unlink_from_conversations!`/Chewy deindex.** Any change to dependent semantics or a new associated table that isn't FK-cascaded silently orphans rows. Highest paging risk.
2. **`Status::SafeReblogInsert._insert_record`** `safe_reblog_insert.rb:20` — monkeypatches AR insert around `deleted_at`. Changing the discard column/teardown invalidates the hand-written SQL.
3. **`discard_with_reblogs`** `status.rb:400` + `before_destroy :unlink_from_conversations! prepend: true` `:178` — two-phase delete ordering; the `prepend` is load-bearing against `dependent: :destroy`.
4. **`default_scope { recent.kept }`** `status.rb:128` — changing discard/visibility flips what **every** query (all of Step 4) returns.
5. **`MediaAttachment` (`dependent: :nullify`)** `status.rb:96` + `RemoveStatusService#remove_media` `:152` + `Vacuum` "media will be orphaned" `:28` — media cleanup is intentionally decoupled; a storage change can leak blobs.

**🟠 High — visibility/distribution gating:**
6. `FanOutOnWriteService` `fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12` (+ `broadcastable?` `:188`, `check_race_condition!` `:38`) — fan-out keys off `visibility`.
7. `StatusReachFinder` `status_reach_finder.rb` — federation targets keyed on visibility + `deleted_at` rewhere `:70`.
8. `RemoveStatusService` `remove_status_service.rb:16` — all `public_visibility?`-gated streaming removals.
9. `StatusPolicy` `status_policy.rb` + `StatusFilter` `status_filter.rb` — if visibility states change, authorization logic must follow.
10. `ActivityPub::Activity::{Create,Delete,Update,Announce,Undo}` (`app/lib/activitypub/activity/*`) — inbound federation create/teardown.
11. `Status::FaspConcern` `fasp_concern.rb` — `after_commit` create/update/**destroy** announce hooks depend on `uri` + `public_visibility?`.

**🟡 Medium — counters, caches, search, derived state:**
12. `increment/decrement_counter_caches` `status.rb:479-493` — `account.statuses_count`, reblog/reply counts; teardown changes can double-count.
13. `StatusCacheHydrator` `status_cache_hydrator.rb:55` + `Cacheable`/`cache_key` `status.rb:190` — `v3:` cache key; payload shape changes must bump it.
14. `Status::SearchConcern` `search_concern.rb` + Chewy deindex in `BatchedRemoveStatusService:38`/`Vacuum:24` — soft-deleted statuses must leave the ES index.
15. `Status::SnapshotConcern` `snapshot_concern.rb` / `StatusEdit` / `UpdateStatusService` — edit history vs. storage change.
16. `AccountStatusesCleanupPolicy` `account_statuses_cleanup_policy.rb` (12 scopes) — automated per-account deletion.
17. `Status::ThreadingConcern` `threading_concern.rb` — recursive SQL on `in_reply_to_id`/`deleted_at`.

**🟢 Lower — read/serialize/admin:** REST + ActivityPub serializers, timeline controllers, `Report`/`AccountWarning`/`Admin::*` moderation reads, `BackupService`, `Form::Import`, presenters, CLI.

**Must be re-verified after the change:** soft-discard then hard-delete leaves no orphans (media, status_stat, conversations, search index, counter caches, redis feeds); reblogs/quotes/replies of a torn-down status resolve correctly; federation `Delete`/`Undo` still reach the right inboxes (incl. `unsafe:` reach); visibility transitions keep `FanOutOnWriteService`/`StatusReachFinder`/`StatusPolicy` consistent; the `_insert_record` SQL patch and `prepend: true` ordering still hold.

result:
I now have a complete, cross-checked picture from the Sense index plus direct reads of the model, concerns, distribution, teardown, and guard code. Here is the audit.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Mastodon — a Rails 8 + React federated social server (ActivityPub). The Sense index shows 4,102 files / 17,571 symbols; Ruby is the backend (`app/`), React/TS is the web client (`app/javascript/`).

**Layout (backend, where the rework lives):**
- `app/models/` — ActiveRecord models + `app/models/concerns/` for shared mixins
- `app/services/` — business operations (post, remove, update, fan-out)
- `app/workers/` — Sidekiq async jobs (distribution, feeds, federation)
- `app/lib/` — domain logic (`activitypub/`, `feed_manager.rb`, finders, vacuum)
- `app/policies/` — Pundit authorization; `app/serializers/` — REST + ActivityPub output
- `app/controllers/` — REST API (`api/v1`, `api/v2`), ActivityPub, admin
- `lib/mastodon/cli/` — maintenance CLI; `spec/` — RSpec + Fabrication fixtures

**The central content model:** `Status` at `app/models/status.rb:34`. It is a top hub (Sense: 124–207 callers, "hub" role). Everything users post is a Status; feeds, notifications, federation, and moderation revolve around it. It is built on a stack of `Status::*` concerns (`app/models/concerns/status/`) and the cross-cutting concerns `Cacheable`, `Discard::Model`, `Paginable`, `RateLimitable`. Its closest collaborators: `Account` (`app/models/account.rb`), `StatusStat`, `StatusEdit`, `Conversation`, `Poll`, `MediaAttachment`, `Mention`, `Favourite`, `Bookmark`, `Quote`, `Tag`.

---

## Step 2 — The Status contract and what it is built on

**Model:** `app/models/status.rb:34`

**Concerns it includes (`app/models/status.rb:35-46`):**
| Concern | File | What it contributes |
|---|---|---|
| `Cacheable` | `status.rb:35` + `cache_associated` `status.rb:180` | fragment/association caching; `CACHEABLE_ASSOCIATIONS` `status.rb:48` |
| `Discard::Model` | `status.rb:36`, `discard_column = :deleted_at` `:66` | **soft-delete** — this is the teardown core |
| `Paginable` | `status.rb:37` | id-based pagination scopes |
| `RateLimitable` | `status.rb:38`, `rate_limit :64` | per-account post rate limiting |
| `Status::FaspConcern` | `concerns/status/fasp_concern.rb:3` | `after_commit` create/update/destroy → FASP federation announce hooks |
| `Status::FetchRepliesConcern` | `fetch_replies_concern.rb:3` | remote reply backfill scopes + `should_fetch_replies?` |
| `Status::SafeReblogInsert` | `safe_reblog_insert.rb:3` | overrides `_insert_record` to block reblogs of discarded statuses at SQL level |
| `Status::SearchConcern` | `search_concern.rb:3` | `indexable` scope, `searchable_by/_text/_properties` (Chewy/ES) |
| `Status::SnapshotConcern` | `snapshot_concern.rb:3` | `has_many :edits`; `build_snapshot`/`snapshot!` for edit history |
| `Status::ThreadingConcern` | `threading_concern.rb:3` | `ancestors`/`descendants`/`self_replies` + `permitted_statuses_from_ids` |
| `Status::Visibility` | `visibility.rb:3` | `enum :visibility` `:7`, `distributable?`/`hidden?`, `set_visibility` |
| `Status::InteractionPolicyConcern` | `interaction_policy_concern.rb:3` | `composed_of :quote_interaction_policy`, quote-permission logic |

**Associations other records hang off (`status.rb:75-118`):** `account`, `application`, `conversation`/`owned_conversation`, `preloadable_poll`/`poll`, `thread`+`replies` (self-join via `in_reply_to_id`), `reblog`+`reblogs` (self-join via `reblog_of_id`), `favourites`, `bookmarks`, `mentions`/`active_mentions`/`mentioned_accounts`, `media_attachments` (`dependent: :nullify`), `tags` (HABTM), `tagged_objects`, `quotes`/`quote`, `preview_cards_status`, `notification` (polymorphic `as: :activity`), `status_stat`, `trend` (`StatusTrend`), `edits` (from SnapshotConcern). Note the **mixed teardown semantics**: `:destroy` (favourites, bookmarks, reblogs, mentions, poll, quote, notification, edits), `:nullify` (media_attachments), `:delete` (preview_cards_status), `nil` (replies, status_stat, owned_conversation, trend).

**Lifecycle the contract guarantees (`status.rb:157-184`):** create-commit hooks (`store_uri`, `update_statistics`, `increment_counter_caches`, webhooks), `before_validation` chain (`set_reblog`/`set_conversation`/`set_local`/`set_visibility`), Snowflake ID assignment `around_create :171`, and **`before_destroy :unlink_from_conversations!, prepend: true` `:178`** (must run before `dependent: :destroy`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:** (1) `default_scope { recent.kept }` `:128` silently hides discarded rows everywhere — changing discard/visibility semantics changes every query; (2) teardown is **two-phase**: soft-discard (`discard_with_reblogs` `:400`) then optional hard `destroy!`, and some children rely on DB foreign-key cascade rather than Rails callbacks (see `BatchedRemoveStatusService`, `Vacuum`); (3) visibility is an enum with `limited`/`direct` special-cased throughout; (4) `proper`/`reblog` indirection (`:234`) — a reblog delegates content to its target.

---

## Step 3 — How a status is created and distributed

**Entry → persistence (inline, synchronous):**
1. `PostStatusService#call` `app/services/post_status_service.rb:42` (from `Api::V1::StatusesController#create`). Idempotency lock `:236`, media validation `:187`.
2. `#process_status!` `:84`: builds `account.statuses.new` `:85` → `ProcessMentionsService` `:86` → tag objects/quote attach `:89-90` → `Antispam` preflight `:92` → `@status.save!` inside a transaction `:97`. Model callbacks fire here (Snowflake id, conversation/visibility setup, counter caches, `store_uri`, FASP create-announce, webhooks).
3. `#postprocess_status!` `:162` enqueues the async fan-out.

**Distribution layers (all async via Sidekiq, enqueued at `post_status_service.rb:163-170`):**
| Layer | Worker → Service | Runs |
|---|---|---|
| Link preview | `LinkCrawlWorker` `:165` | async |
| **Local fan-out** | `DistributionWorker` `:166` → `FanOutOnWriteService#call` `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12` | async |
| Email digests | `EmailDistributionWorker` `:184` | async (delayed) |
| **Federation** | `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker` `:168` → `StatusReachFinder` inboxes → `ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker` | async |
| Poll expiry | `PollExpirationNotifyWorker` `:169` | async (scheduled) |
| Quote request | `ActivityPub::QuoteRequestWorker` `:170` | async |
| Trends | `Trends.tags.register` `:164` | inline |

**Inside `FanOutOnWriteService` (`fan_out_on_write_service.rb`):** race-condition guard `:38`, warms render cache `:170`, then by visibility `:50`: `deliver_to_self!`/`deliver_to_all_followers!`/`deliver_to_lists!` (push `FeedInsertWorker`), notifications (`notify_mentioned_accounts!` `:81` → `LocalNotificationWorker`; `notify_quoted_account!` `:75`), and public streaming via `redis.publish` `:146-163`. Feed writes go through `FeedManager` (`app/lib/feed_manager.rb`). Notifications materialize via `NotifyService` and `PushUpdateWorker`.

---

## Step 4 — Every dependent of the Status contract (grouped, exhaustive)

> Sourced from `sense_graph` callers (153 direct) + `sense_blast` (317 affected symbols, 106 files) + reads. Listed individually.

### Feeds & timelines
- `FanOutOnWriteService#call` `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12` — local fan-out on create/edit
- `FeedManager#clear_from_home` `app/lib/feed_manager.rb:231`, `#clear_from_list` `:251`, `#clear_from_lists` `:271` — feed eviction (also `push_to_home`/`unpush_from_home`/`unpush_from_list`)
- `FeedInsertWorker#perform` `app/workers/feed_insert_worker.rb:7` — inserts status id into a feed
- `Feed#from_redis` `app/models/feed.rb:22` — hydrates status ids from redis
- `PublicFeed` scopes `app/models/public_feed.rb:88,92,96,100,104,108,112,116` — public/local/remote/media/language/account filters
- `TagFeed#tagged_with_any_scope` `app/models/tag_feed.rb:51`, `#tagged_with_all_scope` `:55`, `#tagged_with_none_scope` `:59`
- `LinkFeed#attached_to_preview_card` `app/models/link_feed.rb:42`
- `AccountStatusesFilter` scopes `app/lib/account_statuses_filter.rb:34,48,63,78,82,86,90,94` — per-account profile timelines
- `AccountsController#only_media_scope` `app/controllers/accounts_controller.rb:54`, `#no_replies_scope` `:58`, `#hashtag_scope` `:62`, `#show` `:17`
- `Api::V1::Timelines::HomeController#preloaded_home_statuses` `:31`, `ListController#preloaded_list_statuses` `:27`, `PublicController#preloaded_public_statuses_page` `:31`, `TagController#preloaded_tagged_statuses` `:25`, `LinkController#set_statuses` `:24`
- `TagsController#set_statuses` `app/controllers/tags_controller.rb:47`
- `PushUpdateWorker#perform` `app/workers/push_update_worker.rb:7` — streaming API push
- `StatusCacheHydrator#fill_status_payload` `app/lib/status_cache_hydrator.rb:55` (+ `hydrate_reblog_payload`/`hydrate_non_reblog_payload`) — timeline render cache
- `StatusRelationshipsPresenter#initialize` `app/presenters/status_relationships_presenter.rb:9` — bulk relationship flags for serialization

## Step 5 — How status identity & visibility are guarded

When code operates on a status it confirms **(a) it exists/current, (b) it is visible/distributable, (c) the actor is authorized**:

**Existence / currentness:**
- `default_scope { recent.kept }` `app/models/status.rb:128` — `kept` (Discard) auto-excludes `deleted_at IS NOT NULL`; `.unscoped` is the explicit opt-out used by teardown (`status.rb:402`, `batched_remove_status_service.rb:34`, `vacuum/statuses_vacuum.rb:35`).
- Workers re-fetch by id and rescue `RecordNotFound`: `DistributionWorker#perform` `app/workers/distribution_worker.rb:12`, `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker#perform` `app/workers/activitypub/distribution_worker.rb:16`.
- `FanOutOnWriteService#check_race_condition!` `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:38` — re-queues if `visibility` is nil (partial object).
- `Status::SafeReblogInsert._insert_record` `app/models/concerns/status/safe_reblog_insert.rb:20` — DB-level `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL` so no reblog of a discarded status can be inserted.
- Distributed lock `with_redis_lock("distribute:#{id}")` in both `DistributionWorker:9` and `RemoveStatusService:22` serializes create vs. delete.

**Visibility:**
- `Status::Visibility#distributable?` `app/models/concerns/status/visibility.rb:31` (public||unlisted), `#hidden?` `:27`, scopes `distributable_visibility`/`list_eligible_visibility`/`not_direct_visibility` `:12-14`. Used as gates in `FanOutOnWriteService#broadcastable?` `:188`, `RemoveStatusService#remove_from_public/#remove_from_media/#remove_from_hashtags` (`public_visibility?` checks `:124,135,144`), `StatusReachFinder#distributable?` `app/lib/status_reach_finder.rb:96`, `Status::FaspConcern` (`public_visibility?` `fasp_concern.rb:17,29,36`), `BatchedRemoveStatusService#unpush_from_public_timelines` `:83`.

**Authorization / per-viewer filtering:**
- `StatusPolicy#show?` `app/policies/status_policy.rb:4` — checks `author.unavailable?`, mention requirement, private/following, blocks/domain-blocks. Plus `reblog?`/`favourite?`/`quote?`/`destroy?`/`update?`.
- `StatusFilter#filtered?` `app/lib/status_filter.rb:11` — delegates to `StatusPolicy#show?` `:70` and layers block/domain-block/mute/silence. Drives thread visibility in `Status::ThreadingConcern#permitted_statuses_from_ids` `app/models/concerns/status/threading_concern.rb:14`.
- `StatusReachFinder#followers_scope` `:104` + `inboxes_without_suspended_for` `:114` — strips suspended accounts unless `unsafe:` (delete reach `remove_status_service.rb:98` deliberately passes `unsafe: true`).
- Quote permission: `Status::InteractionPolicyConcern#quote_policy_for_account` `interaction_policy_concern.rb:20`.

**Identity resolution (only "real, local, current" objects act):** `StatusFinder#status` `app/lib/status_finder.rb:10` (verifies route + local URL), `ActivityPub::Activity#status_from_uri` `app/lib/activitypub/activity.rb:70`, `ResolveURLService#process_local_url` `app/services/resolve_url_service.rb:93`.

## Step 6 — Blast radius of the contract change

Sense `sense_blast` rates `Status` **high risk** — 153 direct callers, **317 affected symbols across 106 files**, 1,442 edges. Ranked by likelihood the rework (storage / visibility / teardown) breaks them:

**🔴 Critical — most likely to break, hardest to detect:**
1. **`BatchedRemoveStatusService#call`** `batched_remove_status_service.rb:34` & **`Vacuum::StatusesVacuum`** `vacuum/statuses_vacuum.rb:30` — **bypass Rails callbacks and `delete_all`, relying on DB foreign-key cascade + manual `unlink_from_conversations!`/Chewy deindex.** Any change to dependent semantics or a new associated table that isn't FK-cascaded silently orphans rows. Highest paging risk.
2. **`Status::SafeReblogInsert._insert_record`** `safe_reblog_insert.rb:20` — monkeypatches AR insert around `deleted_at`. Changing the discard column/teardown invalidates the hand-written SQL.
3. **`discard_with_reblogs`** `status.rb:400` + `before_destroy :unlink_from_conversations! prepend: true` `:178` — two-phase delete ordering; the `prepend` is load-bearing against `dependent: :destroy`.
4. **`default_scope { recent.kept }`** `status.rb:128` — changing discard/visibility flips what **every** query (all of Step 4) returns.
5. **`MediaAttachment` (`dependent: :nullify`)** `status.rb:96` + `RemoveStatusService#remove_media` `:152` + `Vacuum` "media will be orphaned" `:28` — media cleanup is intentionally decoupled; a storage change can leak blobs.

**🟠 High — visibility/distribution gating:**
6. `FanOutOnWriteService` `fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12` (+ `broadcastable?` `:188`, `check_race_condition!` `:38`) — fan-out keys off `visibility`.
7. `StatusReachFinder` `status_reach_finder.rb` — federation targets keyed on visibility + `deleted_at` rewhere `:70`.
8. `RemoveStatusService` `remove_status_service.rb:16` — all `public_visibility?`-gated streaming removals.
9. `StatusPolicy` `status_policy.rb` + `StatusFilter` `status_filter.rb` — if visibility states change, authorization logic must follow.
10. `ActivityPub::Activity::{Create,Delete,Update,Announce,Undo}` (`app/lib/activitypub/activity/*`) — inbound federation create/teardown.
11. `Status::FaspConcern` `fasp_concern.rb` — `after_commit` create/update/**destroy** announce hooks depend on `uri` + `public_visibility?`.

**🟡 Medium — counters, caches, search, derived state:**
12. `increment/decrement_counter_caches` `status.rb:479-493` — `account.statuses_count`, reblog/reply counts; teardown changes can double-count.
13. `StatusCacheHydrator` `status_cache_hydrator.rb:55` + `Cacheable`/`cache_key` `status.rb:190` — `v3:` cache key; payload shape changes must bump it.
14. `Status::SearchConcern` `search_concern.rb` + Chewy deindex in `BatchedRemoveStatusService:38`/`Vacuum:24` — soft-deleted statuses must leave the ES index.
15. `Status::SnapshotConcern` `snapshot_concern.rb` / `StatusEdit` / `UpdateStatusService` — edit history vs. storage change.
16. `AccountStatusesCleanupPolicy` `account_statuses_cleanup_policy.rb` (12 scopes) — automated per-account deletion.
17. `Status::ThreadingConcern` `threading_concern.rb` — recursive SQL on `in_reply_to_id`/`deleted_at`.

**🟢 Lower — read/serialize/admin:** REST + ActivityPub serializers, timeline controllers, `Report`/`AccountWarning`/`Admin::*` moderation reads, `BackupService`, `Form::Import`, presenters, CLI.

**Must be re-verified after the change:** soft-discard then hard-delete leaves no orphans (media, status_stat, conversations, search index, counter caches, redis feeds); reblogs/quotes/replies of a torn-down status resolve correctly; federation `Delete`/`Undo` still reach the right inboxes (incl. `unsafe:` reach); visibility transitions keep `FanOutOnWriteService`/`StatusReachFinder`/`StatusPolicy` consistent; the `_insert_record` SQL patch and `prepend: true` ordering still hold.
